expectations.evidence: Title is 'Discovery Brief: Enterprise Bulk Export Functionality' with a structured sprint plan, objectives, hypotheses, interview guide, and success criteria. While not following the exact skill format, it serves the discovery brief purpose.

expectations.text: QUESTIONS TO ANSWER are ranked by priority
expectations.evidence: Section 2 'Discovery Objectives' lists 6 numbered questions. While not explicitly labeled 'ranked', they are ordered from most fundamental (job-to-be-done) to more specific (segments), implying priority ordering.

expectations.text: Methods section specifies interview or analysis methods appropriate for enterprise research
expectations.evidence: Sprint Plan section details methods: customer interviews (45 min each), support ticket analysis, competitive audit, CRM analysis, synthesis workshop. Interview guide with 15 specific questions is included.

expectations.text: Sample section defines who to talk to with minimum n= target
expectations.evidence: Sprint plan specifies 'Recruit 8-10 enterprise customers for interviews (mix of requesters + non-requesters)'. Section 8 'Team & Roles' further defines stakeholder involvement.

expectations.text: OUT OF SCOPE section explicitly lists what is not being explored
expectations.evidence: Section 10 'Out of Scope' lists 5 explicit exclusions: building/prototyping, delivery timelines, non-enterprise segments, data deletion/GDPR erasure, and real-time streaming.

expectations.text: SUCCESS CRITERIA are evidence-based, not activity-based
expectations.evidence: Section 7 'Success Criteria' includes evidence-based criteria: 'We know the top 2-3 jobs-to-be-done', 'We have validated or invalidated each of the 5 hypotheses with evidence', 'We have a clear segmentation'. However, some criteria like 'rough effort estimates' are output-based rather than purely evidence-based.
